summary: ProjZ.py deserializes attacker-controllable pickle data over plaintext HTTP and execs a bundled opaque blob at import time
details: This package exposes installers to remote code execution through two independent, high-impact channels. First, projz/api/control/rpc.py hardcodes RPC_SERVER = 'http://deepthreads.ru' (plain HTTP) and, for every RPC call made by the client (reachable from Client._register, activate, and other user-facing flows), calls pickle.loads() on the raw HTTP response body. pickle.loads on attacker-controlled bytes is equivalent to arbitrary code execution on the installer's machine; the cleartext transport means any on-path attacker, hostile Wi-Fi/ISP, or takeover of the non-first-party domain deepthreads.ru can trivially deliver a malicious pickle and execute code in the context of whatever process uses this library. Second, projz/api/secret/__init__.py performs `exec(dill.loads(b85decode(open('data.txt').read())), secret_functions.__dict__)` at import time (triggered by the top-level `import projz` chain via client.py -> api.headers ->..secret). Running dill/pickle.loads then exec'ing the resulting code objects on every import, with the payload shipped as an opaque base85-encoded blob, defeats source review and is a textbook obfuscation-plus-exec evasion pattern. The combination of import-time exec of an opaque bundled blob plus pickle-over-HTTP RCE from a personal-looking.ru domain is not a pattern present in legitimate client libraries.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
